symptoms.symptom_name: Forceful blunt right chest trauma (horse kick) ~2 hours ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-energy direct blunt force to the chest is the classic mechanism for intrathoracic vascular/parenchymal injury.
Same mechanism can produce tension pneumothorax.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pleuritic breathlessness worsening on the injured side

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lung compression by pleural blood produces unilateral ventilatory failure and pleuritic pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor, cold clammy diaphoresis, dizziness and near-syncope on sitting up

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reported proxies for haemorrhagic shock — postural near-syncope indicates significant volume depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shock disproportionate to visible external blood loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Concealed cavity haemorrhage — a hallmark of massive haemothorax; the collapse is internal.
Simple rib fractures do not cause haemorrhagic shock.
Cerebral hypoperfusion from shock — a pre-arrest marker of severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stony-dull percussion note over the right hemithorax

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dullness reflects fluid (blood) in the pleural space; distinguishes haemothorax from the hyperresonance of pneumothorax.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent breath sounds on the right

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blood between lung and chest wall abolishes breath sounds over the collection.
Low JVP reflects hypovolaemia from blood loss into the chest.
Tension pneumothorax and tamponade both DISTEND neck veins; flat veins argue against them.
Tamponade raises venous pressure (distended veins); flat veins argue against it.
Marked contralateral tracheal deviation is expected in tension pneumothorax; a midline trachea argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension and tachycardia (BP 85/50, HR 130) with SpO2 88%, RR 32

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemorrhagic shock with hypoxia — sealed clinician-measured vitals; via chat the AI infers shock from reported pallor/dizziness, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: eFAST ultrasound showing a large right pleural fluid collection

investigation_details.investigation_name: Extended Focused Assessment with Sonography in Trauma (eFAST)
investigation_details.expected_result: Large anechoic/echogenic fluid collection in the right pleural space
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Delayed recognition of the haemothorax and its size.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of pleural fluid confirms haemothorax at the bedside.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest radiograph — homogenous right opacification with a meniscus sign

investigation_details.expected_result: Homogenous opacification of the right hemithorax with a meniscus sign / blunted costophrenic angle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic radiographic appearance of pleural blood.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Absent breath sounds + stony-dull percussion + flat neck veins + haemorrhagic shock after blunt chest trauma is the classic lethal tetrad distinguishing haemothorax from tension pneumothorax and tamponade.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapid bedside confirmation of a pleural blood collection — but a trauma-team/ED action, never something the AI Doctor performs or awaits before escalating.
Confirms and quantifies the haemothorax after arrival; must not delay the 000 call.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Any home/telehealth attempt to measure or examine before escalating
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do NOT delay 000 to gather vitals, percuss, auscultate or arrange imaging — none are obtainable via chat and delay is fatal.
